---
title: "Compression and Representation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "cgs"
  - "rgs"
  - "rankfold_neuralfold"
  - "rankfold_compressor"
  - "bbvca_v9"
  - "bbvca_main"
  - "bugbrain"
  - "simulation_scaling"
  - "treellm"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Compression and Representation

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `cgs`, `rgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold`, `rankfold_compressor`, `bbvca_v9`, `bbvca_main`, `bugbrain`, `simulation_scaling`, `treellm` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from the handoff outline and source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The architecture needs ways to preserve useful generative structure while reducing surface detail, cost, and memory burden.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Compression can appear efficient while discarding critical semantics, increasing residual burden, or harming downstream utility.

## Core claim

[11-core, support: argument] Compression is a first-class stack layer only when it exposes residual burden, fallback paths, and downstream utility tests.

## Mechanism

- Separate semantic, model, memory, artifact, and representation compression.
- Use generate-verify-repair loops where reconstruction matters.
- Route to fallback systems when probes show compression is inadequate.

## Interfaces

- VCM compresses context with evidence.
- Routing selects compressed or full specialists.
- Evidence tests reconstruction quality and utility.

## Invariants

- Lossy claims remain labeled.
- Residual burden is visible.
- Fallback is available when compressed representations fail.

## Failure modes

- Hidden residual complexity.
- False lossless claims.
- Latency or utility regressions after compression.

## Minimal implementation

A compression ledger with description cost, residual burden, reconstruction checks, and fallback criteria.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Reconstruction quality test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Residual burden test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Probe-and-route fallback test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Compression ratio test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Latency cost test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Downstream utility preservation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `cgs` | Compact Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Smallest adequate structure that can generate/govern target without hiding residual complexity. |
| `rgs` | Ratcheting Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Bridge between active compression, procedural memory, benchmark frontiers, verified AI growth. |
| `rankfold_neuralfold` | RankFold + NeuralFold | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Tensor/artifact compression. Low-rank residual coding plus functional preprocessing and probe-route fallback. |
| `rankfold_compressor` | rankFold compressor | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Alternate RankFold/NeuralFold source. |
| `bbvca_v9` | BBVCA_v9_final_public_release | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Prefer v9. Generate-verify-repair compression from seeded local laws, bounded search, two-phase rate discipline. |
| `bbvca_main` | Big Bang Volumetric Compression Architecture | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Earlier/main BBVCA family doc. |
| `bugbrain` | BugBrain | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Edge/resource-aware implementation context. |
| `simulation_scaling` | Simulation Scaling Law | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Resource constraints on scope, clockspeed, and fidelity in simulations. |
| `treellm` | TreeLLM | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Hierarchical semantic token system for grounded, efficient, explainable language modeling. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest the assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
